results.name: the default boot ('/') routes to the ONE canonical shell
the canonical shell is served from OS2 (os/usr/share/frame/shell.html)
the served shell IS the spatial holospace shell (tab strip · omnibar · app canvas)
the rEFInd boot loader hands off to the shell
the boot chain interposes the greeter for the shell (rEFInd → SDDM → shell)
the greeter's default session + fallback target the shell
holospace.html folds a normal app link into the shell
no duplicate DEFAULT shell in the catalog (a kept self-contained app is exempt)
the old duplicate shell CODE is retired (holospaces/browser · holospaces/sdk · apps/sdk)
the retired shells are unreachable — no fallback serves them (kept self-contained apps exempt)
the ONE shared embed guard exists in the OS runtime (holo-embed.js)
no app renders its own tab strip when embedded — each carries the embed guard (no double-drawn frame)
the shell is sealed in the OS-wide κ-route closure (a did:holo content address)
the served shell RE-DERIVES to its sealed κ — tamper-refused (Law L5)
the shell resolves BY CONTENT at /.holo/sha256/<κ> (substrate-addressable, not path-only)
the shell loader is Secure-Boot-pinned (rEFInd re-derives + refuses a mismatch, Law L5)
the shell is anchored on the unified UOR substrate (carries its did:holo:blake3 σ-axis κ)
the served shell RE-DERIVES to its substrate κ via OS2 BLAKE3 (≡ substrate kappa(), Law L5 / CC-51)

spec: Hologram OS — one canonical holospace shell (shell.html, in OS2): the default and only launcher for every app; duplicates retired; the embed guard enforced for every app
